steps.title: Use the shaded region directly
steps.content: The question asks which ordered pair is a solution to the graphed inequality. A solution is any point that lands inside the shaded region, so there is no need to write the boundary equation.

steps.title: Check each plotted choice
steps.content:
• A $\left(0,-4\right)$ is below the boundary and outside the shaded region.
  • B $\left(0,4\right)$ is on the wrong side of the shaded region.
  • C $\left(-4,0\right)$ is outside the shaded region.
  • D $\left(4,0\right)$ is inside the shaded region.

steps.title: Find the solution point
steps.content: Only $\left(4,0\right)$ is represented by a point in the shaded region. That confirms D is correct.
